title: Velvety Irish Barley Casserole
description: A malty, chewy one-pot Irish barley dish sweetened with maple syrup and brightened by fermented kimchi, served solo for a satvik-friendly dinner.
instructions: Heat the fondue pot over medium heat and add the olive oil.
Sauté the thyme and rosemary until fragrant, about 30 seconds.
Add the barley and stir to coat in the herbs and oil.
Pour in the water and bring the mixture to a gentle boil, then reduce to a low simmer.
Stir in the maple syrup, sea salt, and black pepper, ensuring the syrup dissolves completely.
Cover the pot and let the barley cook for 45 minutes, stirring occasionally to prevent sticking.
After 45 minutes, add the fermented kimchi and stir to combine.
Continue simmering for an additional 10 minutes until the barley is tender and the liquid is absorbed.
Stir in the lemon juice and adjust seasoning if needed.
Serve hot, ladling the casserole directly from the fondue pot.
